headline: SSPDF deploys troops in Akobo for first time since 2013; ~100K fled to Ethiopia; 99 wells destroyed
summary: Radio Tamazuj reports (March 15) that SSPDF physically deployed troops with vehicles and military equipment into Akobo town on Sunday, marking the first government control since civil war erupted in December 2013. County Commissioner James Kueth Makuach ordered armed residents to keep weapons at home and 'move freely in a civilian manner,' warning defiers would be 'held individually accountable.' ReliefWeb Flash Update No. 12 (March 13) confirms ~100,000 individuals crossed into Ethiopia since the March 6 SSPDF evacuation order. 99 wells in Akobo reportedly destroyed or contaminated. Akobo Hospital vacant. 7 of 15 health facilities non-operational. No humanitarian assistance has reached displaced populations. Strongest signal: Reach (R) — 100,000+ cross-border displacement affecting two countries, with total humanitarian access collapse.

headline: Kenya ICT CS Kabogo Presses TikTok for Misinformation Safeguards Ahead of 2027 Elections at Safer Internet Summit
summary: Kenya's ICT Cabinet Secretary William Kabogo pressured TikTok executives at the Safer Internet Summit to strengthen misinformation detection tools ahead of the 2027 elections. The government is proactively seeking platform commitments to counter disinformation campaigns that could disrupt the electoral process.
actors: Kenya Ministry of Information, Communications and Digital Economy
Ambassador Philip Thigo (Special Envoy on Technology)
_original_summary: According to The Star and Kenyans.co.ke, Kenya's ICT Cabinet Secretary William Kabogo officially opened the TikTok Safer Internet Summit 2026 in Nairobi on March 10, 2026, using the occasion to demand clear assurances from TikTok on its tools to detect and counter misinformation and coordinated disinformation campaigns ahead of the 2027 general elections. According to reporting, Kabogo called on TikTok to align its operations with Kenya's regulatory framework including the Data Protection Act, strengthen content moderation, introduce stricter age-verification, and expand operations in local African languages. The summit — themed '#SaferTogether: Innovation and Safety' — was the third annual Sub-Saharan Africa Government Safer Internet Summit and brought together policymakers, regulators, technology leaders, and civil society from across the region.
_review_notes: Proactive government action to address platform responsibility for election integrity, indicating official concern about digital manipulation in upcoming polls.

UNMISS's presence in Akobo was reported in real-time via social media by people on the ground.

headline: Forus International Documents AI-Generated Deepfake Disinformation Threat to Kenya and Five Other African Nations Ahead of 2027 Elections
summary: According to Forus International's March 2, 2026 report, Kenya is among six African nations (alongside Nigeria, Angola, Gambia, Equatorial Guinea, and the Republic of the Congo) facing an AI-generated disinformation threat ahead of their 2027 elections, as deepfake content has grown 550% globally between 2019 and 2023. According to the report, Kenya specifically experienced 17 major coordinated disinformation campaigns in 2025 (per a Trust Lab/EU-funded study), including an AI-generated video smear campaign targeting Dorcas Gachagua (163,000+ views), a forged technical note falsely attributed to the International Foundation for Electoral Systems claiming voter influence had shifted to minority counties, and deepfake content targeting the Kikuyu community. The report warns that 60% of Africa-targeted disinformation campaigns are foreign-sponsored, with AI tools enabling low-cost, high-reach manipulation that erodes electoral trust, amplifies ethnic tensions, and disproportionately targets female politicians through fabricated sexual imagery.

headline: General Johnson Olony apologizes for 'spare no lives' remarks after public backlash
summary: On approximately 4-5 February 2026, General Johnson Olony formally apologized for his 'spare no lives' remarks, stating 'I regret those words.' Speaking after government forces had regained control of parts of Jonglei, Olony acknowledged that his earlier statement telling soldiers to 'spare no one, not even a chicken' was 'reckless and unnecessary.' He announced a disarmament campaign and called on SPLA-IO to 'seek power through elections.' The apology came after condemnation from parliamentarians, civil society, international human rights organizations, and the UN.

headline: SSPDF airstrike destroys MSF Lankien hospital; 26 MSF staff missing; Pieri facility looted
summary: On February 3, 2026, an SSPDF airstrike destroyed the MSF hospital in Lankien, Nyirol County, South Sudan, while the MSF facility in Pieri was simultaneously looted by unidentified assailants. This coordinated attack on medical infrastructure left 26 of 291 MSF staff missing and uncontactable, forcing evacuation of the remaining personnel and cutting critical healthcare access in an already volatile region.

headline: Africa Check Debunks Social Media Disinformation Claiming US Pressured Kenya to Halt China Trade Talks
summary: According to China Global South Project, Africa Check published a formal fact-check on February 2, 2026 finding no evidence to support a social media claim that the United States had pressured the Kenyan government to halt trade talks with China. According to the report, Africa Check traced the origin of the false claim to a misrepresentation of a report in The Standard, one of Kenya's major newspapers. The disinformation spread as a social media meme and carried geopolitical implications, falsely framing Kenya's trade posture as subject to US coercion in the context of US-China economic competition on the African continent.

headline: Kenya Court Withdraws Hate Speech Case Against MP Salasya After NCIC-Supervised Peace Campaign Compliance
summary: According to Eastleigh Voice, the Milimani Magistrate's Court on February 2, 2026 allowed the withdrawal of a hate speech case against Mumias East MP Peter Salasya, with Senior Resident Magistrate Paul Mutai confirming sufficient evidence of compliance with court-mandated conditions. According to the report, Salasya had been charged on May 19, 2025 with making inflammatory remarks on social media allegedly targeting members of a specific community, amounting to hate speech and incitement. Compliance conditions — overseen by the National Cohesion and Integration Commission (NCIC) — included an active peace initiative reaching at least 1,000 people, a sustained peace campaign on social media, and the issuance of a public apology.

headline: Kilifi Foreign Billionaire Beach Eviction Hoax: False Facebook post claims entire beachfront sold
summary: A fabricated Facebook post claimed a foreign billionaire had purchased Kilifi's entire beachfront and would evict local squatters by month's end. The false narrative caused economic disruption as women stopped investing in gardens and kiosks, fearing demolition, when in reality government surveyors were conducting routine land assessments.

Reality: "Surveyors were actually government officials working on a public bypass road, not a private eviction."
_review_notes: Economic impact disinformation where false eviction claims disrupted local investment and livelihoods, demonstrating tangible harm from land grab narratives.

The operation involves ground forces and aerial bombardments with stated objectives to reclaim captured towns and restore government control.

headline: NUSOJ Report: Disinformation and Clan-Based Manipulation Pose Serious Challenge to Mogadishu's First Local Elections in Decades
summary: The National Union of Somali Journalists reported that disinformation and clan-based manipulation posed serious challenges to Mogadishu's first local elections in decades, with monitoring from November 2025 to December 2025 covering 1,604 candidates competing for 390 seats. The report highlighted vulnerabilities in Somalia's democratic processes as external accounts amplified false narratives during this historic electoral milestone.
extracted_claims: Disinformation targeting Mogadishu local elections
_original_summary: According to a report by the National Union of Somali Journalists (NUSOJ) released January 14, 2026, and covering the monitoring period November 1–December 30, 2025, disinformation and misinformation constituted one of the most serious challenges to the Banadir local council elections — Mogadishu's first direct local elections since 1969, with 1,604 candidates competing for 390 seats across 16 districts. According to NUSOJ, online falsehoods were moderate to high before voting, declined slightly on election day, but surged sharply afterward, fueling polarization and undermining public confidence; key tactics included exploitation of clan identity to discredit the vote without evidence, amplified by algorithms and accounts outside Somalia. Despite the disinformation environment, the elections were assessed as peaceful and orderly overall, though marred by state media bias alongside the information disorder.
_review_notes: Important report documenting systematic disinformation threats to Somalia's first local elections in decades, indicating organized interference in democratic processes.

headline: AFRICOM launches 26 strikes in January 2026 alone — 12x increase vs 2024 — targeting both Al-Shabaab and ISIS-Somalia
summary: AFRICOM conducted 26 airstrikes in Somalia during January 2026, representing a twelve-fold increase from 2024 levels, targeting both Al-Shabaab and ISIS-Somalia across multiple regions including Golis Mountains and areas near Mogadishu and Kismaayo. This dramatic escalation in military operations signals a significant shift in U.S. counterterrorism strategy under the Trump administration.

_original_summary: In January 2026, AFRICOM conducted 26 airstrikes in Somalia, a twelvefold increase compared to 2024 figures, targeting both al-Shabaab and ISIS-Somalia across multiple regions. Confirmed targets included ISIS-Somalia in the Golis Mountains (Jan 1-3, Jan 8-9, Jan 12, Jan 23-24), al-Shabaab in Godane (Jan 3, 60km northeast of Mogadishu), and al-Shabaab near Kismaayo (Jan 18). On January 15, Somali/AUSSOM forces killed 30+ al-Shabaab in a joint operation near Bulunagaad base in Lower Shabelle. On January 18, Puntland forces publicly displayed captured Tanzanian, Ethiopian, Yemeni, Moroccan, Turkish, Saudi, and Syrian foreign ISIS fighters in Bosaso, confirming that 60-70% of ISS operational strength comprised foreign fighters.
_review_notes: Major escalation in military operations likely to generate significant information warfare and propaganda responses from targeted groups.

headline: Kenya records 61 terror incidents in 2025: 15 active shooter attacks, 20 IEDs, 9 kidnappings across northeast counties
summary: Kenya's Annual Report on the State of National Security (covering September 2024-August 2025) documented 61 terror-related incidents including 15 active shooter attacks, 20 IED explosions, and 9 kidnappings. A total of 119 people were affected across the most impacted counties—Mandera, Wajir, Garissa, and Lamu—with 84 security officers and 35 civilians injured. Al-Shabaab's activities included probing attacks targeting security agencies, critical infrastructure, and installations, with the group exploiting security gaps and technology to evolve its tactics.

headline: SPLA-IO launches December offensive in Jonglei; captures Yuai on January 2; 100,000+ displaced in 12 days
summary: On 29 December 2025, the SPLA-IO launched a major offensive across northern Jonglei State, overrunning government positions. On 2 January 2026, SPLA-IO forces captured Yuai, the administrative center of Uror County—a significant strategic blow. The offensive was timed to coincide with the dry season, when improved road conditions facilitated military movement after months of flood-induced immobility. By 9 January, fighting (including airstrikes) had displaced over 100,000 people, primarily women, children, and elderly, in what UNMISS described as some of the fastest displacement ever recorded in the country.

headline: Somalia claims Israel-Somaliland recognition conditioned on Palestinian resettlement; Somaliland rejects as fabricated
summary: Somalia's President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud publicly claimed Israel's recognition of Somaliland was conditioned on Palestinian resettlement in Somaliland, Israeli military bases, and Abraham Accords membership. Somaliland's Foreign Ministry categorically rejected these allegations as fabricated, highlighting a disinformation campaign designed to delegitimize the recognition.
escalation_outcome: Federal fragmentation and bilateral agreement cancellation
extracted_claims: Israel's recognition of Somaliland conditioned on resettlement of Palestinians from Gaza in Somaliland
Israel's recognition conditioned on establishment of Israeli military bases in Somaliland
Israel's recognition conditioned on Somaliland's accession to Abraham Accords
_original_summary: Following Israel's recognition of Somaliland (December 26, 2025), Somalia's President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud publicly claimed recognition was conditioned on: (1) resettlement of Palestinians from Gaza in Somaliland, (2) establishment of Israeli military bases, and (3) Somaliland's accession to the Abraham Accords. Somaliland's Foreign Ministry categorically rejected these claims: "no such proposal exists within any legitimate diplomatic framework." The claims had circulated months before recognition, suggesting preemptive disinformation. This triggered Somalia's January 12, 2026 cancellation of all UAE agreements, UAE-aligned media undermining the Federal Government, and two federal member states rejecting Mogadishu's authority within hours.
_review_notes: High-level government disinformation linking Israeli recognition to controversial Palestinian displacement, potentially designed to inflame Arab/Muslim sentiment against the recognition.

headline: AI Deepfake Audio of Somali Senator Faroole 'Calling for Mogadishu Violence' Spreads to 2 Million Facebook Views in 48 Hours
summary: On December 18, 2025, an AI-generated deepfake audio clip falsely attributed to Somali Senator Abdirahman Mohamed Faroole — a Puntland opposition figure at the Kismayo conference — circulated widely on Facebook claiming he called for 'violence to happen in Mogadishu' and invoked clan hostilities. Within 24 hours the clip reached 1.1 million views, 12,000 shares, and 3,700 comments; by December 20 it had 2 million views. The Somali Journalists Syndicate confirmed the audio was a deepfake after cross-checking with AI voice detection tools. Crucially, the Somali Minister of Defence and a government-backed elder publicly repeated the false claims during campaign speeches before the Banadir elections, further amplifying the fabrication.

headline: UN Security Council unanimously extends Al-Shabaab sanctions regime through November 2026
summary: The UN Security Council unanimously adopted Resolution 2806 (December 12, 2025), extending sanctions on weapons entering Somalia and authorization for member states to intercept arms shipments through November 2026. The resolution acknowledged a technical assessment of the arms embargo (S/2025/673) and maintained pressure on al-Shabaab's weapons supply chains.

headline: Amnesty International: Kenya authorities paid state trolls to suppress Gen-Z protests; deployed AI-generated disinformation and surveillance
summary: Amnesty International released its landmark report documenting systematic state-sponsored tech-facilitated violence against Gen-Z protesters between June 2024 and July 2025. The report documented paid troll networks (earning KES 25,000–50,000/day) countering protest hashtags in real time, AI-generated pornographic images targeting women activists, Islamophobic imagery targeting Somali-Kenyan journalist Hanifa Adan, and evidence of Safaricom-enabled surveillance of protesters. Secretary General Agnès Callamard confirmed this was state-driven, with over 128 deaths, 3,000 arrests, and 83 enforced disappearances across the protest period.

headline: ADDO Documents Coordinated #DogsOfWar and #SmallVotesBigDamage Disinformation Campaigns Surging Ahead of Kenya 2027 Polls
summary: According to the African Digital Democracy Observatory (ADDO), two coordinated disinformation campaigns surged on X (formerly Twitter) in Kenya between May and June 2025, with the report published in November 2025. According to ADDO, the #DogsOfWar campaign (May 21–24, 2025) generated 6,754 mentions and 731,150 views, framing civil society activists — including those deported from Tanzania — as foreign-funded agents (linked to Open Society Foundation/Soros) sowing regional destabilization in service of pro-government narratives; related activity reached 131,728 mentions and 32.7 million views. The #SmallVotesBigDamage campaign (June 5–9, 2025) generated 6,172 mentions and 163,356 views, promoting minority ethnic/constituency voting blocs over traditional ones following the Gachagua impeachment, with DISARM framework analysis revealing tactics including fake documents, impersonation of major media outlets, and orchestrated amplification by pro-government accounts.

headline: BBC exposes UAE-linked fake account network: 47,000+ posts portraying UAE as benevolent in Somalia while attacking Hamas and Muslim Brotherhood
summary: BBC investigation (October 2025) exposed a UAE-linked fake social media account network producing over 47,000 posts from January 2023 to September 2025, reaching an audience exceeding 215 million users. The network simultaneously attacked Hamas and the Muslim Brotherhood. Misbar fact-checking confirmed the operation used coordinated multilingual content. This manufactured consent campaign operated for 33 months before exposure, directly preceding the January 2026 Somalia-UAE sovereignty crisis.

summary: UNMISS published its quarterly brief on violence affecting civilians for April-June 2025, documenting 334 incidents affecting 1,518 civilians (635 killed, 676 injured, 133 abducted, 74 CRSV victims). Compared to the previous quarter, violence from conventional parties (SSPDF and armed groups) increased 127%. Compared to Q2 2024, total victims increased 43%. The report highlighted a significant increase in violence attributable to the conflict parties—not just community militias—signaling a qualitative shift in the nature of the violence.

The jailbreak followed just days before the Godka Jilaow prison attack.

headline: EUAA 2025 Country Focus: Al-Shabaab recruitment through coercion, Koranic schools, and clan mediation throughout 2025
summary: The European Union Agency for Asylum's 2025 Country Focus on Somalia documented that al-Shabaab continued multidimensional youth recruitment through 2025: using Koranic schools for indoctrination (particularly in Mogadishu outer districts with high IDP populations), clan mediation as leverage (demanding recruits as payment for conflict resolution services), economic coercion, and ideological appeal. About 500 children were estimated recruited in 2023 alone (UNICEF); the practice continued through 2025. Al-Shabaab also exploited Somali anger at the Ethiopia-Somaliland MOU from 2024 for new recruitment. Puntland government complained that the FGS was 'covertly recruiting Puntland youth' for frontline deployment, creating inter-state recruitment tensions.

headline: Shahada News Agency reports AQAP Gaza aid in August 2025, weaving Somalia-Palestine jihadist narrative
summary: On August 19, 2025, Shahada News Agency — al-Shabaab's Arabic-language propaganda arm — published a report about AQAP sending humanitarian aid to Gaza, featuring a spokesperson for the obscure 'Ahrar Beit al-Maqdis' group. This content, as documented by FDD's Foundation for Defense of Democracies, sought to link al-Qaeda's East Africa branch (al-Shabaab) with the Palestinian cause, building recruitment appeal among Muslims globally grieved by the Gaza conflict. Shahada News Agency had been systematically using pro-Palestinian and Gaza content since October 2023 to expand al-Shabaab's appeal beyond Somalia to an international jihadist audience.

headline: African Stream — RT's Covert Pan-African Media Operation — Shuts Down After Stanford Exposure and Platform Bans
summary: African Stream, exposed by the Stanford Internet Observatory in September 2024 as RT's covert Pan-African influence pipeline, ceased operations by July 1, 2025, citing deplatforming by YouTube, Meta (Facebook/Instagram), TikTok, and Google services. The platform — which claimed to be an independent pan-African media with 2 million followers — had been secretly run by RT with CEO Ahmed Kaballo and at least five reporters holding extensive Russian state media backgrounds. Following US Secretary of State Blinken's September 2024 public exposure, Big Tech platforms enforced bans, cutting its revenue and operational capacity. African Stream migrated to Telegram and Rumble before shutting down.

The fall of Tardo to al-Shabaab on July 14 followed without resistance. Al-Shabaab immediately set up roadblocks, collected taxes, and began governing Moqokori, with propaganda images showing militants engaging with local residents. By July 24, the group controlled a strategic triangle of Moqokori, Tardo, and Buq-Aqable encircling central Somalia.
_review_notes: Al-Shabaab's rapid establishment of governance structures after military victories supports their legitimacy narrative and territorial control claims.

headline: DW/ARD Investigation: Somali 'War Influencers' in Germany Inciting Violence, Raising Funds for Weapons via TikTok and Facebook
summary: A joint investigation by Deutsche Welle and ARD revealed that Germany-based Somali diaspora influencers 'Kabtan Ayub' (Ayub Abdirizak) and Yacqwub Siyaad were using TikTok, Facebook, and YouTube to incite clan violence in Somalia, raise weapons funding, and glorify warfare — all while residing in Germany. Ayub, with hundreds of thousands of followers, gave tactical combat advice in Somali including orders to 'shoot any person on the highway' in military attire. Yacqwub called for followers to 'cut off their heads' and 'slaughter the enemy' while soliciting gold donations from diaspora women for arms purchases. Puntland military court sentenced Yacqwub in absentia to 10 years for misinformation in January 2025. German police investigations were underway as of publication.

headline: Ethnic panic narratives spread during Jonglei/Upper Nile fighting as misinformation dominates social media
summary: CIPESA monitoring from June-November 2024 found that of 255 recorded incidents on South Sudanese social media, 50.5% contained misinformation or disinformation and 39.9% was classified as hate speech. Facebook accounted for 89.4% of harmful content. During the 2025 Jonglei/Upper Nile fighting, OHCHR High Commissioner Volker Türk reported "rampant misinformation, disinformation and hate speech is ratcheting up tensions and driving ethnic divisions and fear." Specific patterns include: false casualty reports amplified to justify retaliatory violence, ethnic attribution of attacks to entire communities rather than specific armed actors, and fabricated content claiming mass atrocities to mobilize diaspora support. The digital space became "a parallel arena of conflict where ethno-nationalist narratives and disinformation further entrench divisions and erode prospects for reconciliation."
escalation_outcome: Direct driver of ethnic violence and displacement
extracted_claims: Ethnic panic narratives during Jonglei/Upper Nile fighting
_original_headline: Misinformation and hate speech drive ethnic panic during Jonglei/Upper Nile fighting; 50.5% of online content classified as disinfo
_review_notes: This represents active disinformation spreading during conflict, not just reporting about it - the 50.5% misinformation rate indicates ongoing false narratives.

headline: BBC Investigation: RT and Sputnik Expand Across Africa While Offering Journalist Training That Dismisses Ukrainian War Crimes as 'Fake News'
summary: BBC Global Disinformation Unit reported on June 26, 2025 that RT and Sputnik had significantly expanded their African operations while facing Western bans, including RT opening bureaus in Algeria, launching a Serbian service, and announcing an India office. Most concerning for East Africa: Russian training courses for African journalists were documented to dismiss Syrian chemical weapons attacks and Ukranian civilian massacres in Bucha as 'fake news,' with participants later adopting these false narratives. These journalists then returned to work in East African media environments, embedding Russian false narratives in regional reporting.

headline: Gen-Z anniversary protests: 16 killed, 400+ injured; government deploys state trolls and internet shutdown orders
summary: On the first anniversary of the June 25, 2024 parliament storming, hundreds of thousands protested across 27 counties under hashtags #RutoMustGo, #OccupyStateHouse, and #SiriNiNumbers. Security forces killed at least 16 people and injured over 400 using live ammunition, tear gas, and water cannons. The Communications Authority ordered a halt to all live television and radio coverage, and Telegram was restricted. Interior Minister Murkomen labeled the protests 'terrorism disguised as dissent,' while state-paid troll networks countered protest hashtags in real time with #RutoMustGoOn.

headline: AI-generated deepfake video fabricated showing President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud burning Somali Constitution
summary: A fabricated video purporting to show President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud burning a copy of the Somali Constitution circulated on Facebook. PesaCheck analysis using DeepFake-O-Meter confirmed 100% likelihood the video was AI-generated. This was part of a series of AI deepfakes targeting the Somali president: another fake video showed him "feasting" during drought (100% AI-generated per DeepFake-O-Meter), and a doctored video showed him claiming "criticism of my family is a crime" (also 100% AI-confirmed fabrication). Additionally, a manipulated photo showed him in clerical garb after Trump's election victory. These coordinated deepfake attacks represent a systematic AI-powered disinformation campaign targeting Somalia's head of state ahead of 2026 elections.
escalation_outcome: Erosion of trust in political leadership; AI-enabled political manipulation
extracted_claims: President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud burned the Somali Constitution
President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud was feasting during drought
_review_notes: Sophisticated AI deepfake campaign targeting Somalia's president represents escalation in digital manipulation tactics.

headline: UN Security Council extends UNMISS mandate; US warns South Sudan 'on brink of broader civil war'
summary: The UN Security Council extended the UNMISS mandate for one year (until April 2026) by a vote of 12-0 with 3 abstentions (China, Russia, Pakistan). The US representative warned South Sudan was 'on the brink of a broader civil war,' citing aerial bombardments, the indiscriminate use of barrel bombs, an attack on a hospital, and the detention of First Vice President Machar. The resolution demanded all parties immediately cease fighting and called on the transitional government to cooperate with UNMISS.

ISS has an estimated 700-1,500 fighters with 60-70% being foreign nationals.

headline: SSPDF helicopter gunships bomb MSF hospital and market in Old Fangak; 7 killed, 20 injured
summary: At 4:30 a.m. on 3 May 2025, two SSPDF helicopter gunships bombed the MSF pharmacy at Old Fangak hospital in Jonglei State, burning it to the ground, then fired on the town for 30 minutes. A drone subsequently bombed the Old Fangak market. At least seven people were killed and 20 injured. The hospital was the only secondary health facility for 110,000+ people. MSF had shared GPS coordinates with all conflict parties prior to the attack. The bombing was the most high-profile attack on healthcare infrastructure during the 2025 escalation.

headline: Al-Shabaab detonates IED targeting President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's convoy in Mogadishu
summary: On March 18, al-Shabaab militants planted a roadside bomb near the Ceel-gaabta junction in Xamar-Jajab district, targeting President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's motorcade as he traveled to the airport. The bomb killed at least 10 people including seven presidential guards and a journalist (Radio Risaala's Mohamed Abukar Dabaashe), and injured 20 others. The president escaped unharmed and continued to the frontlines. Al-Shabaab immediately claimed responsibility via Shahada News Agency and Telegram, framing the attack as an operation to eliminate the 'apostate president' — the first direct assassination attempt on Mohamud since 2014. The attack drew international condemnation from the Arab League, OIC, and UN Secretary-General.

headline: Government falsely attributes Nasir White Army attack to Riek Machar's SPLA-IO command; independent research contradicts prosecution case
summary: Following the March 2025 White Army attack on SSPDF barracks in Nasir, Upper Nile, the South Sudan government publicly blamed SPLA-IO leader Riek Machar, charging him with murder, treason, and crimes against humanity (September 11, 2025). However, a Small Arms Survey report based on interviews with White Army members found that "Riek Machar and the SPLM/A-IO leadership were not responsible for the March 2025 assault" and that "the central argument of the government's legal team is unfounded." The White Army is documented as "an amalgamation of community militias" rather than SPLA-IO-commanded forces. The false attribution narrative was amplified through state media and SSPDF military statements, while UPDF statements equated "SPLA-IO, also known as the White Army" — a factually incorrect conflation. This disinformation serves to justify Machar's prosecution and legitimize military operations in Nuer areas.
escalation_outcome: Treason prosecution of VP, ethnic military operations, near civil war

_review_notes: Clear government disinformation campaign falsely attributing White Army actions to opposition leader, contradicted by independent research.

headline: African Commission Adopts Resolution 630/2025 Condemning Meta's Content Moderation Rollback
summary: The African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights adopted landmark Resolution 630/2025 at its 82nd Ordinary Session, expressing deep concern over Meta's elimination of third-party fact-checkers and warning that reduced platform oversight endangered human rights in Africa. The resolution specifically cited the risk of AI deepfakes, foreign political interference, hate speech amplification, and disinformation during elections and conflict situations. The Commission mandated development of African-specific platform guidelines, underscoring that Meta's changes disproportionately harmed Africa given linguistic blind spots in automated moderation.

Machar wrote to the UN, AU, and IGAD on 23 March accusing Uganda of conducting airstrikes on civilians.

headline: False claim President Ruto hospitalized in critical condition and rushed abroad for emergency treatment
summary: A viral video montage claimed President William Ruto was in critical condition and rushed to Aga Khan Hospital in Nairobi before being flown abroad for treatment. Africa Check verified the claim as false — the video compiled previously debunked clips spliced together to create a new false narrative. The false health claim is part of a pattern of weaponized health rumours targeting political leaders in East Africa (similar to PCCA's claims about President Kiir's health in South Sudan). Such claims are designed to create political instability and test succession narratives.

headline: Anti-Turkey disinformation claims Turkish military in Somalia is "siphoning natural resources"; leaked MIT report documents the campaign
summary: Leaked Turkish intelligence (MIT) report documented sophisticated disinformation campaigns portraying Turkey's presence in Somalia as "purely militarised or extractive" — claiming Turkey is siphoning Somalia's natural resources. Turkey officially denied claims, stressing "mutually beneficial cooperation." Nordic Monitor reported the MIT assessment warning Somalia is "entering its most fragile period in a decade" with disinformation threatening stabilisation. The anti-Turkey narratives, suspected as Gulf-rival-linked, exploit Somalia's fragile media ecosystem where verification capacity is low. Turkey responded by launching TRT Somali in March 2025 as a Somali-language counter-narrative channel.
escalation_outcome: Undermining stabilisation partnerships through narrative attacks

This announcement predated the November 2025 revelation of TASS's full 54-country ambition. The expansion positions Russia for narrative dominance in East African news environments where Russian state agencies may become primary international wire services, particularly in Swahili and Amharic language environments.

Puntland authorities publicized the capture as operational success, while ISIS networks attempted to minimize its significance through online channels.

headline: Fake Infotrak poll fabricated showing Matiang'i leading 2027 presidential race, Ruto third
summary: A fabricated opinion poll purporting to be from Infotrak Research and Consulting circulated on Facebook claiming ex-minister Fred Matiang'i was leading the 2027 presidential race with Ruto coming third. Africa Check confirmed no such poll exists — Infotrak did not release any poll matching the claims. The fake poll was part of a broader pattern of fabricated endorsements and opinion polls targeting Kenya's 2027 election, including forged graphics depicting foreign presidents signalling voting preferences. Africa Check identified election disinformation as the top trend threatening Kenya's democratic process, with deepfake technology increasingly used to create fake endorsements.
escalation_outcome: Undermining electoral integrity ahead of 2027 polls
extracted_claims: Fred Matiang'i leading 2027 presidential race with Ruto third place according to fake Infotrak poll
_review_notes: Early 2027 election manipulation through fabricated polling data, targeting both incumbent and opposition figures.

headline: Isiolo Investor Land Grab Hoax: False WhatsApp claim nearly causes government office arson
summary: False information circulated through a WhatsApp group claiming "an investor associated with some powerful politician had illegally acquired communal land" in Isiolo. FGD participant (ISIOLO-011-001): "Despite the information being false, people had come together in solidarity to go fight and reclaim the land. This caused so much anger that the youth almost burned down a government office."
escalation_outcome: Near-arson of government office; community mobilization for violence

sources.publisher: FGD ISIOLO-011-001 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: An investor associated with a powerful politician illegally acquired communal land in Isiolo
_review_notes: Land-related disinformation proving highly inflammatory in pastoralist communities, nearly triggering violent confrontation with government.

headline: Isiolo youth radicalization pipeline: Economic hopelessness exploited for VE recruitment
summary: ISIOLO-011-006: "The fear that the hopelessness of the youth may be used by Violent Extremist groups to recruit them into VE and gang groups... The youths here feel they have nothing to lose, so they listen to anyone who promises them a better life." Post-Gen Z protest police harassment in Isiolo created additional anger, and gang group formation is reported as an emerging phenomenon in Isiolo town.
escalation_outcome: Gang formation; VE recruitment pipeline

sources.publisher: FGD ISIOLO-011-006 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

_review_notes: Economic desperation creating exploitable conditions for both VE recruitment and gang formation in Isiolo County.

headline: Shakahola cult generalization: "All small evangelical churches in Kilifi are cults"
summary: Following the Shakahola tragedy (Pastor Mackenzie), a generalization narrative spread: KILIFI-003-005: "There is a message going around that 'All small evangelical churches in Kilifi are cults.' This has made some Christians who go to these churches very sad or disgusted. It has made neighbours stop trusting each other. People are being called 'devils' or 'cults' just because they pray in a certain way."
escalation_outcome: Religious discrimination; erosion of inter-faith trust
extracted_claims: All small evangelical churches in Kilifi are cults
_review_notes: Shakahola tragedy creating harmful generalizations about religious communities, damaging social cohesion in coastal regions.

headline: Women lured to Middle East with false job opportunities: Human trafficking misinformation in Kilifi
summary: KILIFI-003-005: "Women are victims of offline harassment. Online, young women in Kilifi are being targeted with misinformation about job opportunities in the Middle East, which often turns out to be human trafficking." The narrative exploits economic vulnerability of young women in coastal areas.
escalation_outcome: Human trafficking; physical danger to women

sources.publisher: FGD KILIFI-003-005 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: Legitimate job opportunities available in Middle East for young women from Kilifi
_review_notes: Classic predatory misinformation exploiting economic desperation in coastal Kenya to facilitate human trafficking.

headline: ISIS-Somalia networks reframe US airstrike as 'foreign aggression on Muslim lands,' intensify anti-US recruitment messaging
summary: After AFRICOM's airstrike killed 46 ISIS-Somalia fighters, ISS propaganda networks on Telegram quickly reframed the strike as foreign aggression on Muslim lands, leveraging anti-US grievance narratives for recruitment. Meanwhile, Trump and SecDef Hegseth publicized the strike on social media as a national security victory. Both sides weaponized the same event for competing propaganda purposes.

reach_data.channels: Manjaniq Media + Al Hijratain Radio + Voice of Munasir + multiple discussion Telegram groups — total number not publicly specified in available sources
reach_data.audience: Primarily Somali-speaking diaspora, Oromo/Amharic-speaking Ethiopian Muslims, Kenyan Somali community
reach_data.speed_of_spread: Rapid — propaganda content appeared within days of strikes
reach_data.notable: Since February 2025, supporters reported deletion of primary discussion groups and accounts, indicating Telegram moderation was ongoing; supporters advised using alternate apps (Zangi messenger)

extracted_claims: US AFRICOM airstrike constitutes foreign aggression on Muslim lands
_review_notes: Demonstrates rapid propaganda response cycle where both sides weaponize same kinetic event for competing narratives.

headline: Sisal plantation eviction rumor: WhatsApp voice note in Kitaita language triggers panic
summary: TAITA-006-005: "WhatsApp Voice Notes are the worst. Someone records a message in the Kitaita language saying there is a plan to evict people for a new sisal plantation. Once that hit the family WhatsApp groups, it's impossible to stop." The use of local language (Kitaita) gives the message perceived authenticity, bypassing literacy barriers.
escalation_outcome: Community panic; trust erosion in institutions

sources.publisher: FGD TAITA-006-005 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: Plan exists to evict people for new sisal plantation development
_review_notes: Local language voice notes exploit cultural authenticity to spread land-related panic in family networks.

headline: KWS Conspiracy: "Government values elephants more than Taita people"
summary: Local bloggers and influencers amplify claims that the Kenyan government deliberately allows elephants to destroy Taita farms to displace people from their land. The narrative transforms legitimate human-wildlife conflict into anti-government conspiracy, potentially inciting violence against KWS officers and infrastructure.
escalation_outcome: Attacks on KWS officers; highway blockades

sources.publisher: FGD TAITA-006-001, 005 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: Government values elephants more than Taita people
KWS purposely allows elephants to destroy farms to displace Taita people
_original_summary: TAITA-006-005: "There is a message going around that the government values elephants more than Taita people. They say KWS purposely lets elephants into our farms to displace people from their land. This creates anger and disgust. It makes people want to attack KWS officers or block the highway in protest." Bloggers amplify real incidents: TAITA-006-001: "They take a real problem, like an elephant killing a person, and they turn it into a narrative that the system is against us."
_review_notes: Dangerous escalation where legitimate human-wildlife conflict is weaponized into anti-government conspiracy with potential for violence.

headline: Secret Airstrip Mineral Theft Conspiracy: Claims minerals stolen via hidden Tsavo airstrip
summary: TAITA-006-003: "We hear that the minerals in our hills are being stolen by 'outsiders' (Watu wa Bara) while we remain poor. There is even a belief that there is a secret airstrip in the Tsavo pack where planes come and take the exploited minerals. This has created an identity and grievance narrative. It makes the youth feel like they are being robbed of their birthright."
escalation_outcome: Youth mobilized to block roads or enter private mines

sources.publisher: FGD TAITA-006-003 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: Outsiders are stealing minerals from Taita hills via secret Tsavo airstrip
_review_notes: Resource exploitation conspiracy builds ethnic grievance identity among Taita youth, potentially radicalizing against perceived outsiders.

headline: "Indigenous vs. Outsiders" delegitimization narrative weaponized in Isiolo politics
summary: Politicians in Isiolo systematically promote narratives dividing "indigenous people" from "outsiders," targeting Somali, Turkana, Samburu, Merian, Borana, and Rendille communities. Paid bloggers amplify these divisive messages across social media platforms, contributing to ethnic tensions and potential violence in the region.
escalation_outcome: Inter-community fear and conflict; political exclusion of minority groups

sources.publisher: FGD ISIOLO-011-001, 002, 003, 005 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: Somali, Turkana, Samburu, Merian, Borana, and Rendille communities are 'outsiders' who should be expelled from Isiolo
_original_summary: Politicians systematically produce and amplify the narrative of "indigenous people" versus "outsiders" in Isiolo, targeting Somali, Turkana, Samburu, Merian, Borana, and Rendille communities. ISIOLO-011-003: "The politicians are the ones who have produced and continue to amplify the narrative... openly calling for the expulsion of the people whom they call outsiders." ISIOLO-011-002: "Bloggers who work for politicians... paid to tarnish names of opponents and some communities." County jobs and development politicized along identity lines.
_review_notes: This reflects systematic ethnic delegitimization tactics that could escalate into violence or displacement in Isiolo County's multi-ethnic context.

headline: Samburu collective blame: Entire community accused of cattle rustling regardless of involvement
summary: The Samburu community faces systematic scapegoating for all cattle rustling incidents in Isiolo, regardless of actual involvement. TikTok videos of rustling incidents are edited with inflammatory messaging that encourages collective punishment against Samburu people, creating fear and anger within the community.
escalation_outcome: Generalized fear among Samburu; reactive mobilization

sources.publisher: FGD ISIOLO-011-002 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: All Samburu people are cattle rustlers and should be collectively blamed for any rustling incidents
_original_summary: Systematic targeting of the Samburu community for all cattle rustling incidents in Isiolo, whether they perpetrated them or not. ISIOLO-011-002: "People from the Samburu community are often accused of cattle rustling and targeted whenever a case of cattle rustling occurs. This has elicited the emotions of anger and fear." TikTok videos of incidents are "edited with messaging that encourages the youth to come out and defend their community or pursue the cattle rustlers."
_review_notes: Collective ethnic blame narratives like this historically precede communal violence and displacement in Kenya's pastoral regions.

headline: Trump's first Somalia airstrike kills senior ISIS-Somalia commander and 46 fighters
summary: At President Trump's direction, AFRICOM conducted its first airstrike of his second term on February 1, targeting ISIS-Somalia operatives in the Golis Mountains near Bossaso. Ten strikes targeted cave systems and killed 46 fighters including the deputy to ISIS-Somalia leader Abdul Qadir Mumin, the head of the group's training, and a key recruiter/financier. Secretary of Defense Hegseth personally confirmed the strike, framing it as part of a broader counterterrorism doctrine. This marked the start of a dramatic escalation in US airstrikes that would eventually reach 109+ in 2025 and 26 in January 2026 alone.

headline: Livestock vaccine conspiracy: Kalonzo Musyoka claims government plan uses "gene-modifying drugs" on cattle as "foreign agenda"
summary: Opposition politician Kalonzo Musyoka publicly claimed President Ruto's nationwide livestock vaccination campaign (22M cattle, 23M sheep, 35M goats, 4.3M camels) was a "foreign agenda" to introduce "gene-modifying drugs" and a "deadly experiment" funded by foreign research institutions. The narrative spread via the "My Cow, My Choice" hashtag on social media, with claims that Bill Gates was involved and vaccines would alter cattle DNA to reduce methane emissions. Kenya's Agriculture CS Dr. Andrew Karanja responded that vaccines are manufactured domestically by KEVEVAPI. Africa Check identified this as one of five key disinformation trends unfolding in Kenya in 2025. The false claims directly threatened uptake of a programme combating diseases that cost Kenya billions in livestock losses annually.
escalation_outcome: Farmer resistance to vaccination programme; public health threat to livestock sector
extracted_claims: Kenya's livestock vaccination campaign uses 'gene-modifying drugs' as part of a foreign agenda
Livestock vaccination is a 'deadly experiment' funded by foreign research institutions
Bill Gates is behind Kenya's livestock vaccination campaign
_review_notes: High-profile political figure spreading anti-vaccine disinformation that could undermine critical livestock disease prevention efforts.

headline: NCA orders blanket 30-day ban on social media platforms citing violence-related content
summary: South Sudan's National Communications Authority ordered a 30-day ban on Facebook and TikTok citing spread of graphic content about South Sudanese killings in Sudan. The ban was quickly reversed following immediate civil society outcry from Amnesty International and the Internet Society South Sudan Chapter.

Adjusted from 5 to 3 (delta -2) by LLM-as-judge calibration

headline: #43against1 campaign pits rest of Kenya against Kikuyu community online
summary: A coordinated hashtag campaign #43against1 emerged on X between January 20-25, 2025, deliberately framing Kenya's Kikuyu community as complicit in political capture and pitting them against the rest of Kenya's 42 other communities. This represents early ethnic mobilization for the 2027 elections, reactivating dangerous Luo-Kikuyu fault lines through coordinated bot networks and opposition-aligned influencers.
actors: Unnamed coordinated bot network accounts (many with same creation dates)
Campaign targeted Gachagua and his political allies

extracted_claims: Kikuyu voters are complicit in their community's political capture under President Ruto
_original_summary: Coordinated hashtag campaign #43against1 emerged on X between January 20–25, 2025, framing Kikuyu voters as complicit in their community's alleged political capture under President Ruto. Nation Africa's year-end analysis identified this as one of the clearest early signals of 2027 ethnic mobilization campaigns. The narrative reactivated longstanding Luo-Kikuyu fault lines and was amplified through cross-platform influence operations.
_review_notes: This is a coordinated disinformation campaign using ethnic division narratives, not just reporting about it. The hashtag itself is the vehicle for spreading false framing.

headline: Fabricated IFES memo and forged DCP documents used to push anti-GEMA ethnic targeting narrative
summary: Ethnically charged campaigns vilifying the Kikuyu community pushed narratives of "tyranny of numbers" using fabricated documents: a fake confidential memo purportedly from the Democracy for Citizens Party warning of GEMA bloc "disintegration," and a forged technical note falsely attributed to the International Foundation for Electoral Systems (IFES). The #43Against1 hashtag framed the rest of Kenya against the Kikuyu community. Africa Check identified this as part of "deepfakes, hashtags, and hate" threatening Kenya's 2027 polls. The forged institutional documents represent an escalation from typical social media disinformation to fabricated official communications designed to lend credibility to ethnic targeting narratives.
escalation_outcome: Ethnic polarization ahead of 2027 elections; potential for physical violence
extracted_claims: Fabricated confidential memo from Democracy for Citizens Party warning of GEMA bloc disintegration
Forged technical note falsely attributed to International Foundation for Electoral Systems (IFES)
_review_notes: Sophisticated document forgery operation targeting Kenya's largest ethnic coalition ahead of 2027 elections, using fabricated institutional sources for credibility.

headline: Religious Profiling Audio: Translated claims government targeting religious institutions in Northern Kenya
summary: KII-NCTC-NAT-001: "Circulated audio translated into various local dialects, claiming that the government was profiling specific religious institutions in the North during the recent security operations." Audio originated on Telegram, amplified via WhatsApp voice notes by "unwitting citizens" in family groups. NCTC identified pattern of exploiting security operations to frame the state as anti-religious. Reached "2 million views before fact-check issued."
escalation_outcome: Brief standoff between youth and security officers in border town
extracted_claims: Government is profiling and targeting specific religious institutions in Northern Kenya during security operations
_review_notes: Dangerous exploitation of security operations to spread anti-government religious persecution narratives through local language channels, amplifying extremist messaging.

headline: Wad Madani killings: "Mundukoro" slave-trade slur resurfaces, triggers retaliatory violence
summary: Following South Sudanese killed in Wad Madani, Sudan (Jan 2025), graphic images/videos circulated on Facebook, TikTok, Clubhouse. Historical "Mundukoro" (man who does cruelty, slave-trade era) resurged. Researcher matrix: "led to the killing of at least 16 Northern Sudanese and the burning of locations." Government imposed curfew and social media ban. MTN Free Facebook amplified spread.
escalation_outcome: 16 Sudanese killed; properties burned; government curfew; social media ban
extracted_claims: South Sudanese civilians were systematically killed in Wad Madani by Sudanese forces
_review_notes: Historical ethnic slurs from slave-trade era being weaponized through graphic social media content, directly triggering deadly retaliatory violence and government crackdowns.

headline: Anti-Sudanese riots erupt in Juba and Aweil after reports of killings in Sudan
summary: Videos circulating on social media allegedly showing South Sudanese civilians killed by Sudanese Armed Forces in Wad Madani triggered violent riots in Juba and Aweil, with armed youth attacking Sudanese-owned businesses and killing at least nine people. This represents the first documented case of Sudan's conflict violence spilling over into South Sudan through inflammatory social media content.

headline: BBC report: 'Somalilanders are smitten with Trump' — Project 2025 Somaliland recognition plans spark Somali government alarm
summary: BBC reporting on Somaliland's enthusiasm for Trump's potential recognition plans has amplified tensions, with Project 2025 explicitly calling for US recognition to counter Chinese influence. Somalia's government has publicly warned that any recognition would violate sovereignty and destabilize the region, while diaspora communities mobilize online around the recognition debate.

headline: Network of Paid Kenya Government Bloggers Operates to Manufacture X Trends and Suppress Protest Voices
summary: Amnesty International documented a coordinated government troll network in Kenya operating at least 20 paid operatives earning KES 25,000-50,000 daily to manufacture pro-government X trends and suppress Gen-Z protest voices. The network operated continuously from January-July 2025, with key accounts like @MurimiJeff_ and @essy_2128 identified as central nodes in the operation.
actors: Unspecified 'patient zero' accounts for pro-government hashtags

narrative_families.calibration_note: Confirmed at 4 by LLM-as-judge calibration
Confirmed at 4 by LLM-as-judge calibration

extracted_claims: Government troll network manufactures pro-government trends
Network earns KES 25,000-50,000 per day for manipulation operations
_original_summary: Amnesty International documented testimony from a self-identified member of Kenya's government troll network ('John') confirming a coordinated network of approximately 20 paid operatives earning KES 25,000-50,000 per day to manufacture pro-government trends on X and drown out protest narratives. The network operated continuously from at least January 2025 through July 2025, deploying counter-hashtags (e.g., #RutoMustGoOn vs. #RutoMustGo), mass-identical message posting, and coordinated harassment campaigns. Specific April 2025 campaigns targeted Somali-Kenyan journalist Hanifa Adan after her BBC 'Blood Parliament' interview using Islamophobic imagery — representing intersection of domestic political trolling with anti-minority hate speech.
_review_notes: Significant documentation of state-sponsored information manipulation infrastructure in Kenya with specific operational details.

headline: Government critics disappeared after AI-generated political satire; Gideon Kibet among released abductees
summary: Cartoonist Gideon Kibet and other government critics were abducted after sharing AI-generated political satire criticizing President Ruto in December 2024. Kibet was released January 9, 2025, amid reports that at least 82 critics had disappeared since June 2024, representing systematic suppression of digital dissent.

Confirmed at 4 by LLM-as-judge calibration
Confirmed at 3 by LLM-as-judge calibration

_original_summary: Cartoonist Gideon Kibet ('Bull') and several other critics were abducted after sharing AI-generated images criticizing President Ruto on social media in December 2024; Kibet was released on January 9, 2025, ten days after President Ruto pledged to halt abductions of opponents. CNN reporting confirmed at least 82 government critics had gone missing since June 2024, with some found dead showing signs of torture. The abductions created a severe chilling effect on online expression, particularly targeting those who shared AI-generated satirical imagery of the president.
_review_notes: Documents escalating use of enforced disappearances to silence digital political expression, including AI-generated content.

headline: Puntland launches major Operation Hilaac against ISIS in Cal Miskaad Mountains
summary: Puntland Security Forces launched Operation Hilaac in January 2025 targeting ISIS militants in the Cal Miskaad Mountains, capturing foreign fighters and destroying bases. ISIS responded with their first recorded drone strikes in Somalia and published denial propaganda through official channels.

extracted_claims: ISIS operations in Puntland are being successfully contained
_original_summary: Puntland Security Forces launched an intensified campaign in January 2025 to block all escape routes from ISIS militants hiding in the Al Miskaad mountain range, capturing foreign fighters from Ethiopia, Yemen, and Tanzania and destroying dozens of bases. ISIS reacted with drone strikes on Puntland forces — the first recorded use of explosive-laden drones by ISS — and published denial propaganda in its weekly al-Naba newspaper mocking 'imaginary victories.' IS-Somalia's Manjaniq Media and Al Hijraytain Radio (broadcasting in Somali, Oromo, and Amharic) simultaneously ramped up online recruitment and fundraising via Telegram, targeting Ethiopian and Kenyan Muslims.
_review_notes: Military operation generating competing narratives between government forces and ISIS propaganda channels.

headline: Meta Ends Third-Party Fact-Checking Program, Raising Africa Disinformation Risk
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the elimination of third-party fact-checking programs on Facebook, Instagram, and Threads, replacing them with user-generated 'Community Notes.' Civil society groups across Africa immediately warned this change would amplify disinformation in conflict zones including Somalia, South Sudan, and Kenya, where social media serves as a primary news source.

headline: Tech Against Terrorism issues call for stronger anti-terrorist website enforcement; cites UN Security Council recognition for Somalia work
summary: Tech Against Terrorism published analysis in January 2025 calling for international frameworks to stop terrorist-operated websites, specifically citing al-Shabaab as Africa's most prolific creator of terrorist online content. The organization's work supporting Somalia's government against al-Shabaab's online presence was formally recognized in UN Security Council Resolution 2713.

headline: Blue Economy Ocean Hoax: "Government selling the ocean to foreigners"
summary: False claims circulating in Kilifi coastal communities allege the government is selling Kenya's ocean to foreigners and will arrest local fishermen. Community focus groups report this narrative creates existential fear among fishing communities, deliberately misrepresenting blue economy policies as theft of local resources.
escalation_outcome: Community resistance to blue economy policy; potential for resource conflict

sources.publisher: FGD KILIFI-003-006 (Phase A Kenya FGD Extractions)

extracted_claims: Kenyan government is selling the ocean to foreigners
Local fishermen will be arrested under blue economy policy
_original_summary: False narrative circulated in Kilifi coastal communities claiming "the government is selling the ocean to foreigners and our local fishermen will be arrested." FGD participants (KILIFI-003-006) reported the narrative creates "a feeling of an existential threat — people feel like their source of life is being taken away." The disinformation misrepresents blue economy policy as government theft of coastal resources.
_review_notes: This represents active disinformation targeting vulnerable coastal communities with economically motivated false claims about government policy.

headline: Security analysis identifies diaspora-based digital mercenaries producing inflammatory content
summary: Kenyan security analysis identifies 'digital mercenaries' in the diaspora producing inflammatory content from Europe and North America without facing local consequences. The report also highlights recruitment of women as information couriers due to lower suspicion from security agencies.
escalation_outcome: Remote production of content causing local physical consequences
_original_headline: Digital Mercenaries: Diaspora-produced inflammatory content from Europe/North America
_original_summary: KII-NCTC-NAT-001: "Digital Mercenaries in the Diaspora — people sitting in Europe or North America who produce highly inflammatory content because they don't face the physical consequences of a riot here." Also described: "Women being recruited as couriers of information because they are less suspected by security agencies."
_review_notes: This is intelligence analysis about disinformation operations, not the disinformation itself. Reveals important operational patterns of overseas-based influence campaigns.

Government Strategic Communication Unit countered the narrative; statistics showed recruitment declining.

headline: Al-Kanabi retaliatory narratives: "Burning, evacuation, killing, and looting" revenge calls
summary: Following attacks on South Sudanese nationals in Sudan's Al-Jazirah State, coordinated disinformation campaigns spread revenge narratives using dehumanizing language. Sudanese accounts promoted Arab supremacist framing calling South Sudanese 'slaves,' while South Sudanese accounts responded with counter-narratives calling Sudanese 'colonizers' and 'prostitutes.'

sources.publisher: KII SS011 (Phase A KII Extractions SS011-SS015)

extracted_claims: South Sudanese are slaves who need sticks for control
Sudanese are colonizers and prostitutes
Burning, evacuation, killing, and looting are justified revenge

From Sudanese side: "slave," "don't buy a slave without a stick" (Arab supremacist framing). From South Sudanese side: "colonizers," "prostitutes." Paid/coordinated accounts noted. Government only responded 4 days after street attacks on Sudanese in Juba.

headline: Kenya press freedom deterioration
summary: Freedom House documented Kenya's largest single-year press freedom decline, scoring 58/100 (Partly Free) due to deteriorating media conditions. This assessment reflects systematic restrictions on media operations and journalistic freedom.
escalation_outcome: Reduced press freedom and media independence
_original_summary: Freedom House scored Kenya 58/100 (Partly Free) during review period—largest single-year decline. Deteriorating press freedom conditions.
_review_notes: Press freedom deterioration creates enabling environment for disinformation by reducing independent fact-checking capacity.

headline: Political youth street mobilization networks
summary: Youth at street bases ("mtaani"), boda-boda stages, car washes, maskanis serve as amplification nodes for political incitement. Coordinated recruitment for physical mobilization.
escalation_outcome: Physical mobilization for violence, gang activation
_review_notes: Street-level networks provide infrastructure for rapid amplification of political narratives and potential disinformation campaigns.

headline: BBS Mall owners petition NCIC over Gachagua's fraud claims

headline: Economic harm during conflict targeting women
summary: Women documented as "shock absorbers" during conflict. When narratives lead to riots, men go to street while women absorb economic costs of property loss, business disruption, food insecurity.
escalation_outcome: Female-headed household impoverishment, economic vulnerability
_review_notes: Critical insight into gendered impacts of disinformation-driven conflict, with women bearing disproportionate economic costs when false narratives trigger violence.

headline: WhatsApp clan/family group amplification networks
summary: WhatsApp family and clan groups function as primary amplification networks for incitement. Encrypted channels enable rapid spread of coded language and conspiracy narratives.
escalation_outcome: Rapid domestic amplification of incitement

headline: Kiryandongo refugee camp violence
summary: Violence and clashes reported in Kiryandongo refugee camp involving cross-border refugee populations. The incident highlights tensions within refugee communities that could be exploited by disinformation actors to escalate ethnic or national conflicts.
escalation_outcome: Internecine refugee violence
_original_summary: Violence and clashes in Kiryandongo refugee camp. Cross-border refugee population tensions and conflict escalation.
_review_notes: Refugee camp violence represents vulnerable population manipulation risk, as displaced communities are prime targets for ethnically divisive disinformation.

headline: Eelay-Hariin Bay region conflict
summary: Conflict in Somalia's Bay region between Eelay-Hariin clan groups has prompted South West State authorities to vow accountability measures. This represents part of an escalating pattern of clan-framed conflicts that could be amplified by disinformation narratives.

headline: Al-Shabaab conducted attacks near Dhuusamareeb and other locations in Galgaduud, Gedo, and Middle Shabelle
summary: Al-Shabaab conducted military attacks in multiple Somali regions including Galgaduud, Gedo, and Middle Shabelle. This appears to be a kinetic security incident rather than disinformation activity.

headline: Political gangs attack church congregation over "Wantam" narrative in Kasarani
summary: Political gang violence erupted at a Kasarani church where politicians were promoting the 'Wantam' narrative. Rival political factions now use gang enforcers for protection at rallies, with threats exchanged between opposing politicians over competing narratives.
escalation_outcome: Physical violence at church; political no-go zones established

Both the politicians affiliated to the two narratives now have gang groups whom they move with to protect them during political rallies. There have also been threats issued where some politicians warn their colleagues that they are not allowed to campaign in some regions."
_review_notes: Represents escalation from information warfare to physical violence over political narratives in Kenya.

headline: False claim Kenya government planning to implant microchips in newborn babies via digital ID programme
summary: A previously debunked 2023 claim resurfaced that Kenya's digital ID programme (Maisha Namba) would implant microchips in newborns. The viral content spliced together unrelated clips of President Ruto and Bill Gates to suggest sinister intentions. Separately, a fabricated quote attributed to Health CS Susan Nakhumicha claimed "DNA tests for newborns are now mandatory nationwide" — Africa Check confirmed no such statement was made. These health conspiracy narratives exploit distrust of digital governance and foreign philanthropy, undermining public health programmes and digital identity rollout.
escalation_outcome: Undermining digital ID and public health programme uptake
extracted_claims: Kenya's digital ID programme (Maisha Namba) will implant microchips in newborn babies
Health CS Susan Nakhumicha said DNA tests for newborns are now mandatory nationwide
_review_notes: Recycled anti-vaccine conspiracy narratives targeting Kenya's digital identity program with fabricated official quotes.

headline: Africa Center documents fourfold surge in disinformation: Russia leads with 80 campaigns in 22 countries; Kenya highest domestic rate at >60%
summary: Africa Center for Strategic Studies documented a fourfold increase in disinformation campaigns across Africa since 2022. Nearly 60% of campaigns are foreign state-sponsored, with Russia (~40% of content, 80 campaigns, 22 countries), China (5 multi-regional campaigns via CCP United Front), UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Qatar as primary sponsors. Kenya has the highest percentage of domestically originating campaigns (>60%) with 9 documented campaigns total. East Africa has the second-highest number of documented campaigns continent-wide. ISS African Futures documented how Russia, China, and Turkey all offer training to African reporters as part of influence strategies. Two well-known Russian-linked African disinformation spreaders alone have 28 million social media followers.
escalation_outcome: Documented systemic threat to East African information ecosystems
_review_notes: Critical research documenting the scale and sources of foreign disinformation operations across Africa, particularly Russian influence.

headline: Pravda network injects 3.7M articles into Wikipedia and AI chatbots, embedding anti-Western narratives into East African information supply chain
summary: DFRLab/CheckFirst investigation documented the Russian Pravda network publishing 3.7 million articles across 140+ subdomains in 83+ countries, repurposing Russian state media content. 1,900+ Pravda hyperlinks were embedded in 44 Wikipedia language versions including Swahili, contaminating AI language models (ChatGPT, Gemini, Copilot, Perplexity) that use Wikipedia as primary source material. Key narratives include: "NATO expansion caused the Ukraine war" (believed by >70% of African respondents), "Western sanctions cause food/energy crises" (not Russia's invasion), and anti-Western geopolitical framing. For East Africa, where AI tools are increasingly used for news consumption, this represents systematic poisoning of the information supply chain — users receive Kremlin-aligned narratives through seemingly neutral AI assistants.
escalation_outcome: Systemic contamination of AI-mediated information
extracted_claims: Anti-Western narratives embedded through Russian state media content
_review_notes: Sophisticated Russian operation contaminating foundational information sources (Wikipedia, AI) used across East Africa.

headline: CGTN/Xinhua push "debt trap is a Western myth" narrative dismissing Kenya's $1B/year SGR debt service documented by Auditor-General
summary: Chinese state media CGTN and Xinhua coordinated to dismiss Kenya's documented SGR debt burden as a "Western debt trap myth." Despite Kenya's Auditor-General revealing $963M in total SGR debt service to China Exim Bank, these outlets framed legitimate fiscal concerns as Western propaganda, potentially influencing public discourse on Kenya's debt sustainability.
escalation_outcome: Undermining domestic fiscal accountability debate through foreign state media
extracted_claims: Kenya's Standard Gauge Railway debt concerns are a Western debt trap myth
_original_summary: Chinese state media published coordinated articles dismissing Kenya's Standard Gauge Railway debt concerns as a "Western debt trap myth." CGTN's "The reality behind the Chinese debt trap myth in Africa" and Xinhua columns frame legitimate Kenyan fiscal concerns as Western propaganda. However, Kenya's Auditor-General Nancy Gathungu revealed Kenya owes China Exim Bank $741M in principal, $222M interest, and $41M penalties for 2025-2026 — the country's largest bilateral creditor. Kenya defaulted on SGR loans in October 2022, incurring $10.8M penalties. The SGR still runs at a loss. Xinhua's Nairobi bureau (largest outside Beijing, 37 bureaus across Africa) distributes content through Nation Media Group reaching 28M social media followers. ISS Africa documented how China offers "self-serving training to African reporters" as part of broader narrative control.
_review_notes: Chinese state media systematically countering documented Kenyan government debt figures with propaganda narratives, potentially undermining informed public debate on fiscal policy.

headline: Police are enemies coming to pick our boys - anti-police fear narrative in Voi
summary: TAITA-006-006: "Since the Gen Z protests, we've seen a narrative that the police are enemies of the people. Some youths were shot dead during the protest here in Voi. People share photos of police trucks and say, 'They are coming to pick our boys.' This creates a permanent state of fear."
escalation_outcome: Permanent state of fear; community avoidance of police

extracted_claims: Police are enemies of the people who come to pick our boys
_original_headline: "They are coming to pick our boys": Post-Gen Z police fear narrative in Voi
_review_notes: Community-level disinformation creating persistent fear of law enforcement, potentially undermining police-community relations and rule of law.

headline: Manipulated video falsely shows Putin declaring support for Kenya Gen-Z protests; AI tools used to fabricate Russian connections and discredit protesters
summary: During Kenya's June-July 2024 Gen-Z protests, a manipulated video circulated claiming Putin supported the protests. Africa Check verified the video used genuine Putin footage overlaid with false English subtitles about patriotism and Ukraine — with no actual mention of Kenya. Separately, DFRLab documented that AI tools were used both to discredit protesters AND fabricate claims of Russian involvement (#TruthlessMan). Pro-government trolls claimed Russian interference to delegitimize protesters while pro-Russian accounts claimed solidarity to build influence — both exploiting the same crisis. Amnesty International documented state-paid troll networks (25,000-50,000 KES/day) creating counter-hashtags (#RutoMustGoOn against #RutoMustGo) and framing youth organizers as "traitors, foreign agents or mercenaries."
escalation_outcome: Erosion of trust in information during political crisis
extracted_claims: Putin declared support for Kenya Gen-Z protests
Gen-Z protesters have Russian connections
_review_notes: Sophisticated dual-use AI manipulation both falsely claiming foreign support for protests and discrediting protesters with fabricated foreign connections.

There are people who are unable to distinguish whether such AI generated images are true or false." escalation_outcome: Arrests; mysterious disappearances of sharers

headline: "Thank you Putin for securing Christianity in Kenya": Russian-linked campaign frames Russia as protector of African values against Western cultural imperialism
summary: Russian-linked social media networks promoted religious messaging positioning Putin as protector of Christianity in Kenya against Western cultural imperialism. The coordinated campaign used recruited Kenyan influencers to spread pro-Kremlin sentiment by framing Russia as defender of African Christian values.
escalation_outcome: Deepening social divisions exploiting religious identity for geopolitical gain
extracted_claims: Putin is securing Christianity in Kenya against Western threats
_original_summary: Africa Center for Strategic Studies documented Russian-linked social media campaigns in Kenya using religious framing to build pro-Kremlin sentiment. The narrative "Thank you Putin for securing the fate of Christianity in Kenya" was promoted through coordinated accounts, positioning Russia as defender of African Christian values against Western liberal cultural imperialism — specifically framing Western LGBTQ+ rights advocacy as an attack on African traditions. More than 70% of survey respondents in African countries believed Russian disinformation narratives including "Sanctions against Russia are the main cause for the current food and energy crises" were probably or definitely true. In digitally connected Kenya, pro-Russian messages are disseminated through networks of fake accounts, bots, and recruited local influencers under themes of national pride, often without disclosing Russian connections.
_review_notes: Classic Russian playbook using religious identity to build influence and anti-Western sentiment in Kenya.

headline: "Wantam" vs. "Tutam" ethnic political rhetoric dividing Kenya along tribal lines
summary: NAIROBI-047-003: "The dominant harmful messaging is about 'wantam' (one-term) and 'tutam' (two-term) political rhetoric driven by politicians. This narrative has divided the country along ethnic lines. If you are Kikuyu, people assume you are following Gachagua and you are pushing 'wantam'. If one is Kalenjin, they are presumed 'tutam'." The narrative has generated gang formation and geographic no-go zones: "Here in Kamkunji, there are places you cannot even mention 'Wantam'."
escalation_outcome: Armed gangs; geographic no-go zones; church attacks

_review_notes: Dangerous ethnic polarization rhetoric creating gang formations and geographic divisions in Kenya.

headline: Mogadishu Evictions-to-Recruitment Pipeline: Al-Shabaab frames evictions as "criminal acts"
summary: Al-Shabaab exploits FGS evictions in Mogadishu neighborhoods, framing 2024 public land reclamation as "criminal acts against the people" to delegitimize the government and fuel youth recruitment. Researcher matrix identifies CRITICAL risk with escalation pathway: eviction grievance → AS propaganda framing → youth radicalization → recruitment uptick in Banaadir.
escalation_outcome: Documented recruitment pipeline from policy grievance to VE radicalization
extracted_claims: FGS evictions in Mogadishu are criminal acts against the people
_review_notes: Critical example of Al-Shabaab exploiting legitimate grievances to delegitimize government and fuel recruitment.

headline: FGD report: Politicians arming youth gangs and radicalizing communities with police complicity
summary: Focus group discussion from Nairobi documents politicians radicalizing and arming youth gangs to defend their interests, with attacks occurring during Gen Z protests. These armed groups operate with impunity alongside police at locations including Jacaranda grounds and Jevanjee Gardens.
escalation_outcome: Armed gang attacks at public forums; police-gang coordination
